revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[de:Gohei]][[en:Gohei]][[es:Gohei]][[fa:گوهئی]][[fr:Gohei]][[hu:Gohei]][[id:Gohei]][[it:Gohei]][[ja:御幣]][[ko:고헤이 (제구)]][[ru:Гохэй]][[sr:Гохеи]][[te:గోహేయి]][[tr:Gohei]][[zh:御幣]]
{{Short description|Shinto wand}} {{Italic title|reason=}}{{More footnotes|date=November 2021}}[[Image:Shinto gohei.jpeg|thumb|150px|{{transliteration|ja|Gohei}} in front of Shinto shrine]] {{nihongo|||'''Gohei'''}}, {{nihongo|||'''onbe'''}}, or {{nihongo|||'''heisoku'''}} are wooden {{ill|Wand|en|Wand|qid=Q148493|lt=Wand|1=magic wand}}s, decorated with two {{transliteration|ja|{{ill|Shide (Shinto)|en|Shide (Shinto)|qid=Q2657982|lt=shide|1=Shide (Shinto)}}}} (zigzagging paper streamers) used in [[Shinto]] rituals. It may be considered an {{ill|Ōnusa|en|Ōnusa|qid=Q1153887|lt=Ōnusa|1=Ōnusa}} with only two Shide.
The streamers are usually white, although they can also be gold, silver, jade, or a mixture of several colors, and are often attached as decorations to straw ropes ({{transliteration|ja|{{ill|Shimenawa|en|Shimenawa|qid=Q1887421|lt=Shimenawa|1=Shimenawa}}}}) used to mark sacred precincts.
The [[kannushi|shrine priest]] or attendants ({{transliteration|ja|[[miko]]}}) use the {{transliteration|ja|gohei}} to bless or sanctify a person or object in various Shinto rituals. The {{transliteration|ja|gohei}} is used for some ceremonies, but its usual purpose is to cleanse a sacred place in temples and to cleanse, bless, or exorcise any object that is thought to have negative energy. In addition to its use in [[ritual purification|purification ritual]]s, it may be included in an {{transliteration|ja|{{ill|Ōnusa|en|Ōnusa|qid=Q1153887|lt=Ōnusa|1=Ōnusa}}}} (wooden wand with many {{transliteration|ja|shide}}), and serve as the object of veneration ({{transliteration|ja|[[shintai]]}}) in a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}}. A type of food Called {{ill|Goheimochi|en|Goheimochi|qid=Q10855366|lt=Goheimochi|1=Goheimochi}} is thought to have been named after the staff.<ref>{{cite web |title=Food: The Gohei mochi in the Edo era |url=https://www.discovertajimi.com/goheihistory.html |website=Tajimi Tourism Association |access-date=26 February 2024 |language=en}}</ref> The characters Reimu Hakurei and Sanae Kochiya from {{ill|Touhou Project|en|Touhou Project|qid=Q907907|lt=Touhou Project|1=Touhou Project}} wield gohei.
== See also == * {{ill|Flail|en|Flail|qid=Q1427748|lt=Flail|1=Flail}} * [[Glossary of Shinto]] for an explanation of terms concerning Japanese Shinto, Shinto art, and Shinto shrine architecture. * {{transliteration|ain|[[Inau]]}}, wooden wands used in {{ill|Ainu people|en|Ainu people|qid=Q101828|lt=Ainu|1=Ainu people}} rituals * {{transliteration|ja|{{ill|Gunbai|en|Gunbai|qid=Q1188570|lt=Gunbai|1=Gunbai}}}} * {{ill|Ruyi (scepter)|en|Ruyi (scepter)|qid=Q1448880|lt=Ruyi (scepter)|1=Ruyi (scepter)}} * {{transliteration|ja|{{ill|Saihai|en|Saihai|qid=Q7400093|lt=Saihai|1=Saihai}}}} * {{transliteration|ja|[[:en:Shaku (ritual baton)|Shaku]]}} * {{transliteration|ja|{{ill|Ōnusa|en|Ōnusa|qid=Q1153887|lt=Ōnusa|1=Ōnusa}}}} * {{transliteration|zh|{{ill|Hu (ritual baton)|en|Hu (ritual baton)|qid=Q1185997|lt=Hu|1=Hu (ritual baton)}}}}
==References== {{Reflist}} * {{cite book |title=The Cambridge Illustrated History of Religions |publisher=Cambridge University Press |location=[[New York City]] |first=John W |last=Bowker |year=2002 |isbn=0-521-81037-X |oclc=47297614 |url-access=registration |url=https://archive.org/details/cambridgeillustr00john }} * {{cite book |title=Shinto: Origins, Rituals, Festivals, Spirits, Sacred Places |publisher=Oxford University Press |location=[[Oxford, New York|Oxford, NY]] |first=C Scott |last=Littleton |year=2002 |isbn=0-19-521886-8 |oclc=49664424}} * {{cite book |title=The A to Z of Shinto |publisher=The Scarecrow Press |first=Stuart DB |last=Picken |year=2002 |isbn=0-8108-5586-0 }}
<!--this was an inappropriate and erroneous transclusion meant to be a bottom bar thing I think but not including-->
{{Shinto-stub}}
